narration: One in four people with a 401k has taken a loan from it. You think you're just borrowing from yourself — but here's what banks never tell you.
visual: Dark background. 401k statement SVG showing $80,000 balance with a $20,000 loan taken out. Red LOAN stamp animates in diagonally over the document.

narration: Your 401k contributions went in pre-tax. But when you repay the loan, you're using after-tax dollars — money the IRS already took a cut from.
visual: Light background. Vault SVG in center. Three flow steps appear: green dot for PRE-TAX IN, amber dot for LOAN OUT, red dot for AFTER-TAX REPAYMENT.

narration: Then at retirement, you pay income tax again on that same repaid money. That twenty-thousand loan gets taxed twice. Most people never see it coming.
visual: Dark background. Dollar bill SVG with two red TAX PAID stamps animating over it at different angles — one for repayment, one for retirement withdrawal.

narration: But the double tax isn't even the worst part. Twenty thousand dollars out of your portfolio misses growing into one hundred and fifty-two thousand dollars over thirty years.
visual: Light background. Bar chart with two bars: red '$20K BORROWED' and amber 'LOST VALUE' growing tall. Counter animates up to $152,000.

narration: Lose your job while the loan's still open? The full balance is due immediately — or it's counted as income and hit with a ten percent early withdrawal penalty.
visual: Dark background. Warning triangle SVG in red. Three bullet lines appear: balance due immediately, taxable income, 10% penalty. Bold punch line at bottom.

narration: Before you touch your retirement, try a personal loan, a zero-percent APR card, or a high-yield emergency fund first. Follow for more money moves they never taught you.
visual: Light background. Piggy bank SVG. Three checklist items appear: high-yield emergency fund, 0% APR card, personal loan. FOLLOW CTA badge springs in.
